title: Ticket 04 - Cost, security & sustainability
description: Dummy data for ticket 04 - cost, security & sustainability (V01) in the DoomScrollQuiz.

questions.prompt: Ticket 04 centers on this working idea: "Plan and deliver cost, security and sustainability reliably in day-to-day network operations." Which statement matches it best?

options.text: We should handle cost, security and sustainability in network operations clearly, document decisions, and implement them in a stable way.
options.explanation: This summarizes the core idea behind cost, security and sustainability in network operations in a direct, practical way.

options.text: We can ignore cost, security and sustainability in network operations as long as the rollout feels manageable.
options.explanation: Cost, security and sustainability in network operations should not be postponed or ignored.

options.text: Only external providers are allowed to deal with cost, security and sustainability in network operations.
options.explanation: This makes the topic unnecessarily exclusive and misses the learning goal.

options.text: Cost, security and sustainability in network operations only matters after the service has already gone live.
options.explanation: The learning idea should shape decisions throughout the project and rollout.

questions.prompt: Which reaction best fits ticket 04 when working with cost, security and sustainability in network operations?

options.text: Clarify the goal briefly, document the decision, and make the next rollout step explicit.
options.explanation: This combines alignment, transparency, and actionable implementation.

options.text: Skip documentation first and rely on memory once the rollout gets busy.
options.explanation: For cost, security and sustainability in network operations this would be imprecise and risky.

options.text: Raise issues only after they have already caused a visible outage.
options.explanation: The better direction is to clarify and mitigate risks early instead of reacting too late.

options.text: Avoid questions even if the dependencies are still unclear.
options.explanation: The learning idea requires clarity, not silent guessing.
